observations.recommendation:
1. We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should compel the Inventory Committee to conduct and complete its physical count of all LGU properties as soon as possible, mark property counted assets with property tags and submit all the required Reports on Physical Count thereon with complete details to the Audit Team.
2. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer, shall coordinate with each other in providing information on the cost or fair market value of all LGU’s roads as well as in the reconciliation of its records in order for them to provide accurate Report on the Physical Count of Road Network.
3. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare adjusting entries to correct misclassification of recorded accounts in the procurement of equipment as stated in paragraph 1.9.
4. Furthermore, we recommended that the Property Custodian shall maintain Property Cards for all properties of the LGU and reconcile these with the Reports on the Physical Count of PPE and accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
Physical count had been fully conducted for Land and Land
Improvement and Public Infrastructure accounts while
other PPEs were only partially counted due to
unavailability of property tags and data from the
Engineering Office on the constructed.
The Inventory Committee failed to convene due to the
retirement of the former Municipal Treasurer.
The Property Custodian was not implemented due to the retirement of the Municipal Treasurer.
The Municipal Accountant was unable to prepare Property Ledger Cards for PPEs such as Buildings and Road Networks since the required technical data from the Municipal Engineer was not yet submitted.
The Municipal Engineer likewise explained that he had already submitted a report to the former Municipal Treasurer. Nonetheless, he committed to submit an updated report to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason:
Physical count had been fully conducted for Land and Land
Improvement and Public Infrastructure accounts while
other PPEs were only partially counted due to
unavailability of property tags and data from the
Engineering Office on the constructed.

observations.recommendation:
5. We recommended that the Inventory Committee should conduct a physical count of inventories, prepare and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant on or before July 31, 2021.
6. We further recommended that the concerned personnel-in-charge on the distribution of Drugs and Medicines and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies to submit every end of the month the RSMI supported with duly filled-up RIS to the accounting unit which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the issuances during the month.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant only record the recognition of inventories based on the SSMI and RIS.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was unable to prepare the required reports due to heavy workload.
The Municipal Treasurer admitted she was not able to retain copies of the RIS or prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). She also disclosed that the physical count of inventories on hand was not conducted, hence, the required Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) was not also prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.
Although no physical count was conducted by the Municipality’s Inventory Team, the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) initiated the conduct of physical count of the medicines and medical supplies which were not dispensed as of year-end. A report of the “Rural Health Unit (RHU) of Kawayan Medicines, Medical and Laboratory Supplies Inventory” as of December 31, 2021 was prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant. The said report, which was certified by the MHO, showed the quantity of medicines and medical supplies received, dispensed, and on-hand.
The Municipal Accountant explained that he intended to prepare an adjusting entry to bring the book balances of the Drugs and Medicines and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies accounts equal to the actual balances of inventories on hand as at year-end per RHU report. Instead, he erroneously recorded the said ending balance as an expense. The Municipal Accountant gave his assurance that the same will be immediately corrected.
During the Exit Conference, the Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that they have already discontinued the practice of recording purchases of inventories as outright expense.
The Municipal Treasurer and all the officials concerned gave their commitment to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason:
The physical count of inventories was not conducted due to the workload and conflict of schedule of the members-in-charge.
The required reports were not submitted on a monthly basis due to the workload of the personnel-in-charge.

observations.recommendation:
21. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all Department/Office Heads mandating the following: Receiving items purchased by the LGU shall be centralized and to be done only by the designated Property Officer;
22. Logbook should be maintained by each Department/Office to record all supplies received from the Property Officer, record their receipt and issuances of items; and
23. Submit a report of stocks on hand every June 30 and December 31 each year to the designated Property Officer for consolidation.
We also recommended that the designated Property Officer:
24. Prepare a pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for every item issued to each requisitioning office, duly signed by the Head of office;
25. Summarize all RIS in the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the following month as basis in recording the issuances as expenses;
26. Consolidate the Report of Physical Count of Inventories prepared by each department and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, to tally balances in the accounting records; and
27. Maintain an updated and complete stock cards.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
28. Maintain an updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC); and maintain the SLC; and
29. Reconcile balances in the SLC with the RPCI submitted by the designated Property Officer and prepare valid adjustment in case of discrepancies.
observations.managementAction:
There were still inventories which were recorded as outright expense.
Only the MHO submitted the report of stocks on hand at yearend.
The designated Property Officer was assigned the task after the retirement of the former Municipal Treasurer. However, said Property Officer has no proper training on the assigned task.
The Municipal Treasurer was unable to prepare the necessary documents to open separate bank account for the water system operations due to retirement of the former Treasurer.
The Municipal Accountant was unable to maintain the SLC due to heavy workload.
observations.reason:
There were still inventories which were recorded as outright expense.
The required reports were not implemented due to the retirement of the former Municipal Treasurer and lack of personnel-training and heavy workload.

observations.recommendation:
30. Prepare the necessary documents to open a separate bank account for the LGU-Kawayan water system operations, subject to approval of the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan; and
31. Deposit all collections from water concessionaires to the said Water System bank account.
32. We also recommended that the Municipal Waterworks Office submit monthly the Billing Statements to the Municipal Accountant for recording of the monthly receivables from all water concessionaires.
33. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system depository operations and record therein the monthly billings, collections and the related disbursements.
34. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection of the facilities and structures being used for the water system operations, assess its current values and submit a report thereon to the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to harmonize their respective records.
observations.managementAction:
The separate bank account for the Water System operations was opened.
The Municipal Treasurer deposited collections to the said bank account. However, the Municipal Officer did not submit the Summary of Billing Statements to the Municipal Accountant.
The Municipal Accountant did not prepare the separate book of accounts since no separate account was opened previously.
The Municipal Engineer did not conduct the inspection and process due to COVID-19 travel restrictions and lack of personnel to facilitate inspection and appraisal.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Assessor was unable to travel and process documents due to COVID-19 travel restrictions.
Lack of personnel to conduct inspection and appraisal.
The Municipal Officer did not submit the billing statements; Municipal Accountant did not maintain separate books due to prior unavailability of separate account.

observations.recommendation: 35. Pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for Region VIII, reduce the number of JOs to the most necessary only and to complement the existing workforce of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor reduced the number of JOs and adjusted payments where applicable. However, full compliance was constrained by limited funds of the LGU.
observations.reason: The recommendation was not implemented due to the limited funds of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
41. Prepare the documents needed for the transfer of ownership of the lot purchased for dumpsite and Barangay Health Station as well as other donated lands; and
42. Appraise lands without acquisition cost to facilitate recording of the same in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was unable to travel and process documents due to COVID-19 travel restrictions and the Municipal Assessor lacked personnel to conduct inspection and appraisal.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Assessor was unable to travel and process documents due to COVID-19 travel restrictions.
Lack of personnel to facilitate conduct inspection and appraisal.

observations.recommendation: 43. Maintain Property Cards for all properties of the Municipality; 44. Update PAR of each employee every three years; Request for disposal of unserviceable properties and provide training to Property Officer; 45. The Municipal Treasurer should maintain separate register for every program and record the funds received per program including its related disbursements.
observations.managementAction:
A Property Officer was designated to do the task after the retirement of the former Municipal Treasurer. However, said Property Officer has no proper training on the assigned task.
Not all departmental offices maintained a logbook and recorded their receipts and issuances of supplies.
The Municipal Treasurer prepared the documents and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan. However, no action was taken until his retirement.
The Property Officer was assigned the task after the retirement of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The Property Officer was designated but has no proper training and task implementation was delayed due to retirement of former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
1.17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Create an Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all their regular
duties, to conduct the complete physical inventory in three (3) months or less.
b) Initiate the implementation of all prior years’ audit recommendations on PPE
account, as well as, the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE
accounts” as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
c) Consider the appointment of a General Services Officer (GSO) who shall take
charge of property management.
1.18. We recommended that the Inventory Committee:
a) Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and the submission of a copy to the COA Audit
Team, at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking
activities; and,
b) Conduct physical count and prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE using the
prescribed form and the submission of the report to the Audit Team.
1.19. Further, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal
Accountant prepare and maintain a Property Ledger Card for each asset of the LGU
based on the RPCPPE and all newly procured items.

2.13. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer summarize all the Requisition
and Issue Slips (RIS) issued during the year in a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant, together with the Distribution Sheet,
if any, to support the inventories consumed/issued during the year.
2.14. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant only recognize in the
books the issued/distributed/consumed inventories based on the SSMI, RIS and
Distribution Sheet, if any, submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
2.15. We further recommended that the Department Heads concerned submit to the
Municipal Accountant the duly signed Distribution Sheet to support the recognition of expense of
the distributed food supplies and welfare goods to beneficiaries, if any, on a monthly basis.
2.16. Furthermore, we recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct actual
physical inventory of all undistributed/unconsumed inventory every semester to confirm the
correctness and accuracy of balances in the reports.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry
voucher to record the donated motor vehicle to its proper Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) account and the corresponding depreciation of the asset
pursuant to IPSAS 17.
3.8. We also recommended that for similar transactions in the future, the Municipal
Treasurer secure all related documents of the donated property from the donor
and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.

4.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record all the identified
reconciling items and
b) prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within
ten days from receipt of the Bank Statements.

5.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry to
record the CY 2021 unexpended balance of ₱374,236.70 as Trust Liability-
DRRMF in the Trust Fund books.

6.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Chairperson of the
Bids and Awards Committee to submit to the Audit Team an explanation on the
deficient/defective Performance Security of the contracts presented in Table 12.
6.12. We also recommended that the BAC Chairman require the Contractors to post
additional Performance Security with a validity period that covers another one
year from completion date and to submit a Performance Security with validity
period for those security that were not indicated.
6.13. We further recommended that the BAC Secretariat examine the validity period
of the Performance Security posted by the winning bidders before transmitting
the contract to the Municipal Mayor for signature and ensure that the validity
period is correctly indicated.
6.14. Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor only issue the Certificate
of Final Acceptance one year after the completion of the project.

7.9. We recommended that the Chairperson of the Bids and Award Committee
(BAC):
a) Submit explanation to the Audit Team why date of receipt of the NOA and
the NTP of the six (6) packages of infrastructure projects were not indicated.
b) Henceforth, require the Winning Bidders or Contractors to indicate in the
Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed the date this was received to
determine the timelines and deadlines of each procurement activity.

8.12. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer:
a) Exert best efforts to implement all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP;
b) Submit an explanation on the delayed submission of the monthly utilization
reports; and
c) Henceforth, submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
within the reglementary period.

9.8. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discontinue the practice of
issuing checks in the name of the Barangay Treasurer, rather it should be the
name of the barangay or the official title or designation of the Accountable
Officer as the payee for all payment due to the Barangays.
9.9. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the recipient
Barangays to submit the Fund Utilization Report or Liquidation Reports and
its supporting documents within the agreed period stated in the Memorandum
of Agreement.

7. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer should transfer the remaining balances of assistance to the DRRM- Bank account.
8. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an entry to transfer the remaining balances from Due to NGAs to Trust-Liability DRRM account, prepare subsidiary ledger by source of donor to conform with COA Circular 2012-002 in accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Health Officer to submit the distribution list duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries and/or any evidence to support the actual distribution of drugs and medicines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued directive and the Municipal Health Officer submitted the distribution list duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries.

The Municipal Mayor gave assurance and directives to concerned drivers and officials to adhere to the required trip ticketing and accountability.
The Municipal Treasurer and other officials committed to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
13. We recommended the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why these transactions were indorsed for payment despite the deficiencies of the supporting documents. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.
14. Henceforth, BAC Chairman should ensure compliance with pertinent documentary requirements to avoid audit suspensions or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted justification and the BAC Chairman committed to ensure compliance with documentary requirements in the future.

observations.recommendation:
15. We recommended that the LGU improve its budget allocation and utilization/project implementation to maximize the benefits that its constituents could derive out of its available budget.
16. Also, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should fill-out completely all the required information in the Purchase Order such as the date and the period within which the delivery should be made and the date the PO was received by the supplier.
17. Further, we recommended that the Inspectorate Team should indicate the actual date of inspection and require the suppliers or its representatives to fill-out completely the Delivery Receipt specifically the date of delivery so that liquidated damages for delayed deliveries can be computed and be deducted from the claims by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and Municipal Accountant committed to improve completeness of documentation and monitoring of deliveries and to compute and impose liquidated damages where appropriate.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documentary requirements under COA Circular 2012-001 including the proof that conditions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 were complied with.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant submitted the required documentary requirements and proofs. Validation to be done by COA.

observations.recommendation:
19. Transfer to the Special Trust Fund the actual cash equivalent of the unexpended current year LDRRMF immediately before the end of each year; stop recording intra-agency receivables and payables on the said balances; Trust Fund included in the LDRRMF and prepare consolidated LDRRMFIP.
20. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the MDRRM Council, identify specific programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be funded from the previous years unexpended balances and require the MDRRMO to include the said PPAs under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP to be prepared prior to the approval of the annual budget to ensure that disbursements therefrom are authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction:
Although the actual cash was transferred to the separate depository account immediately before yearend, the same was still not recorded as Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books.
The Municipal Mayor and MDRRM Council initiated steps to identify PPAs and include them in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
36. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all collectors to remit daily all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer.
37. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to monitor such actions based on the RCD submitted to her monthly and render a report to the Mayor in case of non-compliance.
observations.managementAction:
All collectors were able to remit their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Treasurer discontinued the practice of daily remittances to reduce cost and time constraints but committed to deposit collections intact at least twice a week.
The Municipal Treasurer committed to deposit the collections intact, at least twice a week.

The Municipal Treasurer submitted Reports on Collections and Deposits and Collector’s RCD. The Municipal Accountant reconciled and prepared adjusting entries and committed to reconcile cashbook with SL quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain a separate register for every program and record the funds received per program including its related disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the necessary documents and submitted the same to the Sangguniang Bayan. However, said Municipal Treasurer retired and no further action was taken until his retirement.

observations.recommendation: 46. Design a strategy to enhance extensive and effective Tax Collection Campaign program.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: The Local Chief Executive designed and implemented strategies to enhance tax collection and conducted a Tax Collection Campaign Program.

observations.recommendation:
1.14 We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
should compel the Inventory Committee to conduct and complete its physical
count of all LGU properties as soon as possible, mark counted assets with
property tags and submit all the required Reports on Physical Count thereon
with complete details to the Audit Team.

1.15 We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer, the Inventory Committee
and the Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with each other in providing
information on the cost or fair market value of all LGU’s roads as well as in
the reconciliation of its records in order for them to provide accurate Report
on the Physical Count of Road Network.

1.16 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare
adjusting entries to correct misclassification of recorded accounts in the
procurement of equipment as stated in paragraph 1.9.

1.17 Furthermore, we recommended that the Property Custodian shall maintain
Property Cards for all properties of the LGU and reconcile these with the
Reports on the Physical Count of PPE and accounting records.

2.8 We recommended that the Inventory Committee should conduct a physical
count of inventories, prepare and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant
on or before July 31, 2021.

2.9 We further recommended that the concerned personnel in-charge on the
distribution of Drugs and Medicines and Medical, Dental and Laboratory
Supplies to submit every end of the month the RSMI supported with duly filled-
up RIS to the accounting unit which will be the basis of the Municipal
Accountant to record the issuances during the month.

3.7 We recommended the Municipal Treasurer should transfer the remaining
balances of assistance to the DRRM- Bank account.

3.8 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an entry
to transfer the remaining balances from Due to NGAs to Trust-Liability
DRRM account, prepare subsidiary ledger by source of donor to conform with
COA Circular 2012-002 in accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

4.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal
Health Officer to submit the distribution list duly signed by the actual
recipients/beneficiaries and/or any evidence to support the actual distribution
of drugs and medicines.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:
a. explain the usage of gasoline and diesel to activities not related to COVID-
19;
b. require the Drivers to submit duly filled Trip Tickets to support the
payment of the diesel and gasoline; and
c. adhere to pertinent rules and regulations on the utilization of funds
received by the Municipality that is intended for specific purpose.

6.15 We recommended the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why
these transactions were indorsed for payment despite the deficiencies of the
supporting documents. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.

6.16 Henceforth, BAC Chairman should ensure compliance with pertinent
documentary requirements to avoid audit suspensions or disallowance.

7.14 We recommended that the LGU improve its budget allocation and fund
utilization/project implementation to maximize the benefits that its
constituents could derive out of its available budget.

7.14 Also, we recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should fill-out
completely all the required information in the Purchase Order such as the date
and the period within which the delivery should be made and the date the PO
was received by the supplier.

7.15 Further, we recommended that the Inspectorate Team should indicate the
actual date of inspection and require the suppliers or its representatives to fill-
out completely the Delivery Receipt specifically the date of delivery so that
liquidated damages for delayed deliveries can be computed and be deducted
from the claims by the Municipal Accountant.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary
documentary requirements required under COA Circular 2012-001 including
the proof that conditions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 were
complied with.

1.20 We reiterate our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor issue
a directive to Inventory Committee, to enforce and complete the following
actions within the current year:
1.20.1 Complete the physical count of all LGU properties in CY 2020;
1.20.2 Mark counted assets with Property Tags;
1.20.3 Prepare the RPCPPE with complete details; and
1.20.4 Submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant who shall
reconcile the same with her records and provide copy of the report to
the Audit Team.

1.21 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) provide the
Designated Property Officer with the list of tangible items costing ₱15,000.00
and below appearing as PPE in the books for the issuance of ICS to the
persons responsible for the items; and (2) re-classify the said items to the
appropriate Inventory account duly supported with the duly signed ICS.

1.22 In a letter dated July 28, 2020 from the Municipal Accountant, noted by the
Municipal Mayor, both officials expressed that the recommendations were well
taken and gave their assurance to implement the same in CY 2020.

2.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the correct
depreciation of all properties under PPE, except Land, every year and
submit the computation schedule of the annual depreciation of PPEs to the
Audit Team every January of each year.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
3.8.1 Demand from the five Punong Barangays, who received funds from
LGU-Kawayan in prior years, to submit immediately the Utilization
Reports on the use of said funds to prove that it was used for public
purpose; and
3.8.2 Stop the grant of additional financial assistance to Barangays unless
the previous release has been fully accounted.

3.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
3.9.1 Prepare correcting entries on the funds transferred to Barangays by
recording in the Due From Local Government Units account the funds
transferred by the LGU to the Barangays, and;
3.9.2 Inform the Municipal Mayor monthly on unsubmitted Utilization
Reports of Barangays as guide in selecting the Barangay qualified for
additional grant of funds transfers.

4.16 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
4.16.1 Transfer to the Special Trust Fund the equivalent cash of the
unexpended current year LDRRMF immediately before the end of
each year and stop recording intra-agency receivables and payables
on the said balances;
4.16.2 Maintain subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF, both in the General Fund
and in the Trust Fund, especially for transfers and utilization of the
Special Trust Fund by year of transfer and trace back prior years’
transactions in order to arrive at the correct balance of the Trust-
Liability-DRRM account reconciled with the available Cash in Bank
for the said fund; and
4.16.3 Prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization and the Notes to the
Financial Statements with due professional care and diligence in
order to provide the officials of LGU-Kawayan with an accurate
information on the status of its LDRRMF.

4.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the
MDRRM Council, identify specific programs, projects and activities
(PPAs) to be funded from the previous years unexpended LDRRMF in the
Special Trust Fund and require the MDRRMO to include the said PPAs
under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP to be prepared prior to the
approval of the annual budget to ensure that disbursements therefrom are
authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.

5.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
5.9.1 Present as Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position
the principal amount of the loan that is due and payable in the
succeeding year; and
5.9.2 Obtain from the bank the Debit/Credit Memos and support the same
in the Journal Entry Voucher prepared to record the loan payments
and other bank transactions affecting the loan account of the LGU, if
any.

6.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all
Department/Office Heads mandating the following:
6.13.1 Receiving items purchased by the LGU shall be centralized and to be
done only by the designated Property Officer;
6.13.2 Logbook should be maintained by each Department/Office to record
all supplies received from the Property Officer, utilized during the
month and the month-end balances of remaining stocks on hand; and

6.13.3 Submit a report of stocks on hand every June 30 and December 31
each year to the designated Property Officer for consolidation.

6.14 We also recommended that the designated Property Officer:
6.14.1 Prepare a pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for every
supplies issued to each requisitioning office, duly signed by the Head
of office;
6.14.2 Summarize all RIS in the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day
of the following month as basis in recording the issuances as expenses;
6.14.3 Consolidate the Report of Physical Count of Inventories prepared by
each department and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, to tally balances
in the accounting records; and
6.14.4 Maintain an updated and complete stock cards.

6.15 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
6.15.1 Stop recording as expenses the procured supplies and materials unless
it is supported with an SSMI and RIS from the designated Property
Officer;
6.15.2 Maintain an updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC);
and
6.15.3 Reconcile balances in the SLC with the RPCI submitted by the
designated Property Officer and prepare valid adjustment in case of
discrepancies.

7.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council:
7.15.1. identify and include in the AIP projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure its completion;
7.15.2. include in the AIP only the projects that are to be funded from the
20% DF which must be capital in nature and avoid general and
vague terms; and
7.15.3. stop allocating from the 20% Development Fund the cost for rental
of lot for Water System operation and instead, include the same in
the budget from MOOE of the LGU.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
8.7.1 Prepare the necessary documents to open a separate bank account for
the LGU-Kawayan water system operations, subject to approval of the
Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan; and
8.7.2 Deposit all collections from water concessionaires to the said Water
System bank account.

8.8 We also recommended that the Municipal Waterworks Office submit
monthly the Summary of Billing Statements to the Municipal Accountant for
recording of the monthly receivables from all water concessionaires.

8.9 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate
books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations and record
therein the monthly billings, collections and the related disbursements.

8.10 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection
of the facilities and structures being used for the water system operations,
assess its current values and submit a report thereon to the Property Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to harmonize their respective records.

9.5 We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately the
Disbursement Vouchers for the remittance to the different barangays their
respective RPT shares for CY 2019, and henceforth, remit the Barangay RPT
shares every 5th days after the end of each quarter, without need of demand.

10.11 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Require each Job Order worker to sign a Contract of Service which will
indicate the specific tasks to be performed by them, the office of
deployment and other necessary provisions to protect the interest of both
the LGU and the person being hired, to be notarized after the same had
been signed by the Local Chief Executive; and
b.) Pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for
Region VIII, otherwise, reduce the number of JOs to the most necessary
only and to complement the existing workforce of the Municipality.

10.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments of
wages of JO workers based on duly notarized contracts and quantified
accomplishment reports, certified by the Head of the Department where the
JO is assigned, and record the payments correctly in the proper expenditure
accounts.

11.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit to the GSIS, within
the first ten (10) days of each month, the full amount withheld from
employees’ salaries to avoid incurrence of surcharges and provide to the
employees the GSIS benefits which they are entitled to.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive requiring all
Collectors to remit daily all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer and
for the Municipal Treasurer to deposit daily the full amount of his
collections to LBP Naval Branch. Also, require the Municipal Accountant
to monitor such actions based on the RCD submitted to her monthly and
render a report to the Mayor in case of non-compliance.

13.10 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
13.10.1 Submit Reports on Collections and Deposits, supported with
Collector’s RCD, accountable forms issued and deposit slips, to the
Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day of each succeeding
month so the latter could record in the books all transaction on the
same month;
13.10.2 Record in the Cashbook, with extreme care, all collections and
deposits on the actual date of the transactions;
13.10.3 Review all transactions recorded in the Cashbook and correct
immediately any errors found; and
13.10.4 Reconcile cashbook balances with the SL of the Municipal
Accountant at least once every quarter.

13.11 Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the correctness
of the amounts reflected in the Subsidiary Ledgers, reconcile SL balances
with the Cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer quarterly and prepare
adjusting entries to correct the misstated Report of Collections and Deposits
of the accountable officers as shown in Appendix J.

14.7 We recommended that the MDRRMO maintain a Cashbook for his Petty
Cash Fund and Special Purpose Cash advances and record therein all
increases as well as decreases of cash under his accountability based on
source documents. At all times, update daily the Cashbook showing the
totals and balances of the cash under his accountability.

15.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, include in the SWM Plan the
provision for attendance by a qualified LGU personnel in necessary
trainings, preferably those conducted by TESDA, who shall cascade his
learnings among other personnel involved in the collection of solid wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor: (1) prepare the  documents
      needed for the transfer of ownership of the lot purchased for dumpsite
      and Barangay Health Station as well as other donated  lands;  and  (2)
      appraise lands without acquisition cost to facilitate recording of the
      same in the books.


 7.   Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

     a. Record all Land owned by  the  LGU  based  on  the  report  of  the
        Municipal Assessor, including all donated assets;
     b. Record 100% of road network projects in the books in CY 2018  based
        on the report of the Municipal Engineer;
     c. Derecognize the carrying value of projects which has undergone  re-
        blocking or repairs;
     d. Compute  the  Allowance  for  Accumulated  Depreciations  on  every
        depreciable asset instead of the aggregate total amount, except the
        road lot component of the road network system which  shall  not  be
        subject to depreciation;



  8. We also recommended that  all  department  officials  submit  a  report
     immediately to the  Municipal  Accountant  on  all  donated  properties
     received by their respective Offices.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  prepare  the  adjusting
      entries to record the utilized amount of P12 million by  reducing  the
      liability account Due to NGAS after a thorough analysis and review  is
      conducted.


  16. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer  transfer  the  LGSF
      fund immediately after the amount is downloaded to the GF  account  by
      Bureau of Treasury in order to prevent the use of other fund for  LGSF
      purpose.

We recommended that the LDRRMO  together with the LDRRM Council:

     a. Review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include activities to be
        funded from the prior years’  Special  Trust  Fund.   Any  revision
        thereon must bear the approval of the SB.
     b. Submit the LDRRMFIP to COA at the start of the year;
  15. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

     a. Prepare the Report on Receipt and Utilization of  DRRMF  using  the
        prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002  and  avoid  lumpsum
        presentation of amounts;
     b. Reconcile the balances in the Report on Receipt and Utilization  of
        DRRMF with the Trust Liability-DRRM account ledgers;
     c. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF in the General Fund  and  in
        the Trust Fund, the latter by year of transfer.
     d. Include all calamity funds received  from  outside  source  in  the
        Report on Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman  of  the  Local
    Development Council, exclude in the AIP, program and  activities  which
    are NOT capital or investment in nature so  that  the  20%  Development
    Fund  will  be  appropriated  and   utilized   solely   for   long-term
    infrastructure projects.


12. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement all  projects
    in  the  AIP  during  the  year  to  avoid  the   stagnation   of   the
    municipality’s development.


13. We recommended also that the Municipal Treasurer  maintain  a  separate
    register for every program and record the funds  received  per  program
    including its related disbursements.

We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC, the BAC members, the  TWG
    and BAC Secretariat:

    a. Submit the COMPLETE legal and technical documents  of  all  contracts
       for the procurement of goods and infrastructure in CY 2018;


    b. Review strictly and thoroughly the documents of prospective bidders;


    c. Confirm the eligibility documents of prospective bidders;


    d. Allow only qualified bidders to join in the LGU’s bidding, and;


    e.  Submit  to  COA  every  month  the  complete  procurement  documents
       including the integral parts of each contract,  duly  signed  by  the
       contracting parties, and proofs of postings of the Notice  of  Award,
       Notice to Proceed and awarded contracts of the LGU.

7.9   These  recommendations  are  without  prejudice  to  the  issuance  of
    Notices of Suspension  or  Notices  of  Disallowance  on  the  CY  2018
    transactions, when the evidence so warrants.

observations.reason: Lack of personnel to handle records of Property, Plant and Equipment

observations.recommendation: 4.)We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statements and revert the stale checks on the month that an accurate and reliable cash balances in the financial statements are presented.

5.)We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit always all collections to the LGU’s depository banks to avoid exposure of these funds to possible loss or misuse.

6.)We recommended that the Municipal Accountant with the approval of the Local Chief Executive, send demand letters to the officers and employees of the Municipality with unliquidated cash advances, requiring the immediate submission of their liquidation documents or refund the amount of cash advance. 

7.)Impose penal sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002 should these employees refuse to heed the demand letters.  

8.)Further, do not grant additional cash advances to employees pending liquidation of existing accounts.

9.) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that Contracts/Purchase Orders entered into by and between the LGU and Contractors/Suppliers contain the contract duration of the project and the expected delivery dates for the procurement of goods, as guide in contract implementation and at the same time protect the interest of the LGU from delayed completion of projects or delivery of goods.

10.)We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Development Council to exclude in the Annual Investment Plan for the 20% Development Fund,  PPAs which are administrative in nature.

11.)We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant stop utilizing the 20% Development Fund to pay only transactions which are administrative in nature.
observations.reason: The Municipal Development Council formulated the Annual Investment Plan for the 20% Development Fund in 2016 while the guidelines was issued in 2017. Hence, expenses which are administrative in nature were already excluded in the succeeding 20% EDF and MDC will strictly implement the provision of DILG/DBMJMC No. 2017-1

observations.recommendation:
18.)We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the LDRRM Fund are utilized only for PPAs specifically prescribed under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. 

19.) Henceforth, subsequent procurement of Gasoline and Diesel and reimbursement for training and travels for purposes other than disaster prevention, preparedness and mitigation should be charged against the general fund of the LGU.

24.) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all members of the Local School Board ensure that programs and activities for Early Childhood Care Development are incorporated in its budget for SEF in accordance with Republic Act No. 10410 and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s, 2017.

25.) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal SWM Focal Person to design the SWM plans in measurable and specific terms, implement fully its targets within the year and utilize the SWM budget efficiently.

29.) We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all necessary information regarding the projects being undertaken by the Municipality shall be fully supplied in the prescribed signboards.  

30.) Also, ensure that a List of all on-going government PPAs and those for implementation during the year are submitted to COA at the beginning of the year.

observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is not the approving authority for loan application of employees but the Municipal Treasurer. Upon receipt of the AOM No more approvals of loan for employees below the minimum amount of Four Thousand Pesos (P4,000.00) however, there are employees with below the minimum amount required due to existing loan before AOM was received.

observations.recommendation:
34.)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all employees exercise prudence in filing claims for official travels and observe strictly the existing provisions of Executive Order 248 and 298.

35.) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the completeness of the supporting documents and ensure the legality, correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before payments for traveling expense are approved.   

36.) Henceforth, require the submission of ORs as basis to support claims for taxi fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose.

37.) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to review meticulously the correctness of the entries in the JEV, approve the same and use the JEVs as bases of recording in the Journals.  

38.) Also, post the balances of controlling accounts in the respective Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) instead of utilizing the Journals as SL. 

39.) Henceforth, abide with the basic accounting principles and provisions as embodied in Chapter 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 2.

40.) We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit always all financial reports and trial balances within the prescribed timeline. 

41.) Also, submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid penal sanctions. 

42.) Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose the penal provisions in the afore-cited law and COA Circulars when the said officials fail to render accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the Accounting Office and the Accountant who is loaded with the activities in the Accounting Office is also the BAC Secretarial resulted to the delayed preparation of the report.

observations.recommendation:
43.) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point to coordinate with the offices/divisions concerned to ensure that the programs/projects identified in the GAD Plan are fully implemented.  

44.) Likewise, direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenses that are attributed as GAD-related activities actually address GAD-related issues and concerns.

45.) We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents.

46.)We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain SubsidiaryLedgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the Budget Officer.
observations.reason: Some projects included in the AIP were not implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation:
47.) We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit the authority granted by the SB and MDRRMF prior to procurement of vehicle.

48.) Also, include in the MDRRMFIP all items to be  procured and activities to be undertaken out of the Mitigation Fund and TrustFund and disburse said funds only if found in the MDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Utilization of LDRRM Fund for repairs and maintenance exceeded its allocated amount in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation: 49.)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the LCE, should act with urgency in the mandatory closure of the existing open dumpsite as he may be held liable for violating the solid waste management law.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already submitted its Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan to EMB 8-DENR.
observations.reason: Non- implementation yet of Perimeter Fencing and establishment of compost pits.

observations.recommendation:
50.) We recommend that the BAC should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law.

51.) Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is on the process of preparing its PMR and ensure its submission to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law.
observations.reason: There is no Organic employee in charge in the preparation of the PMR. The BAC Secretariat who is also a SWO II, was loaded with activities in the MSWD Office resulted to delayed preparation of the report.

observations.recommendation: 52.) We recommend to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on thesubmission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already instructed the person in charge to prepare and submit the required report.
observations.reason: Officer in-charge in the preparation of the report is also the e-SRE focal person, hence failed to submit report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the valid book reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statements and revert the stale checks on the month that an accurate and reliable cash balances in the financial statements are presented.
Management committed to abide with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit always all collections to the LGU’s depository banks to avoid exposure of these funds to possible loss or misuse.
The Municipal Treasurer asserted that the undeposited balance every month end was caused either by the late remittance of the collectors or non-availability of motor vehicle to be used in going to and from the bank while the yea- end balance was caused by the numerous holidays.
It was pointed out that the undeposited amount were already recorded in the books hence the funds were already remitted by the collectors to the Municipal Treasurer.  We maintain that the Municipal Treasurer must deposit regularly and intact his collections to avoid exposing the same to possible loss or misuse.
The LCE agreed that trips to the bank by the Municipal Treasurer will be given priority.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant with the approval of the Local Chief Executive, send demand letters to the officers and employees of the Municipality with unliquidated cash advances, requiring the immediate submission of their liquidation documents or refund the amount of cash advance. Impose penal sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002 should these employees refuse to heed the demand letters.  Further, do not grant additional cash advances to employees pending liquidation of existing accounts.
Management informed the Audit Team that the outstanding Advances under the SEF were liquidated in the first quarter of 2018.  The Audit Team insisted that demand letters must be issued to the officials and employees whose cash advances are long overdue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that Contracts/Purchase Orders entered into by and between the LGU and Contractors/Suppliers contain the contract duration of the project and the expected delivery dates for the procurement of goods, as guide in contract implementation and at the same time protect the interest of the LGU from delayed completion of projects or delivery of goods.
Management accepted the recommendations and committed to implement the same henceforth.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Development Council to exclude in the Annual Investment Plan for the 20% Development Fund, PPAs which are administrative in nature.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant stop utilizing the 20% Development Fund to pay only transactions which are administrative in nature.
The recommendation was well taken by Management and assured the Team that the budget for CY2018 will be corrected and tailored in accordance with the afore-cited Memorandum Joint Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the LDRRM Fund are utilized only for PPAs specifically prescribed under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. Henceforth, subsequent procurement of Gasoline and Diesel and reimbursement for training and travels for purposes other than disaster prevention, preparedness and mitigation should be charged against the general fund of the LGU.
Management agreed to abide by the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all members of the Local School Board ensure that programs and activities for Early Childhood Care Development are incorporated in the LSB budget for SEF.
Management explained that due to limited funds of SEF, the School Board  concentrated on the necessary expenditures and overlooked programs for ECCD for children’s 4 years and below.   However, the Municipal Mayor assured that they will allocate funds for ECCD in the SEF Budget for 2018 and revisit the Circular on the Revised Guidelines on the use of SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal SWM Focal Person to design the SWM plans in measurable and specific terms, implement fully its targets within the year and utilize the SWM budget efficiently.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all necessary information regarding the projects being undertaken by the Municipality shall be fully supplied in the prescribed signboards.  Also, ensure that a List of all on-going government PPAs and those for implementation during the year are submitted to COA at the beginning of the year.
The management agreed to follow the required posting/signage specifically the pertinent information of the projects

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all employees exercise prudence in filing claims for official travels and observe strictly the existing provisions of Executive Order 248 and 298.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the completeness of the supporting documents and ensure the legality, correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before payments for traveling expense are approved.
Henceforth, require the submission of ORs as basis to support claims for taxi fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to review meticulously the correctness of the entries in the JEV, approve the same and use the JEVs as bases of recording in the Journals.  Also, post the balances of controlling accounts in the respective Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) instead of utilizing the Journals as SL.  Henceforth, abide with the basic accounting principles and provisions as embodied in Chapter 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit always all financial reports and trial balances within the prescribed timeline. Also, submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid penal sanctions.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose the penal provisions in the afore-cited law and COA Circulars when the said officials fail to render accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point to coordinate with the offices/divisions concerned to ensure that the programs/projects identified in the GAD Plan are fully implemented.  Likewise, direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all expenses that are attributed as GAD-related activities actually address GAD-related issues and concerns.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment on the completed physical inventory to the Municipal Accountant.  Also, mandate the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records and investigate discrepancies noted.
We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendations requiring the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of PPE every year, and submit a report on the results thereon.  We further recommend that the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory  Committee
to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and  Equipment
on the completed physical inventory  to  the  Municipal  Accountant.   Also,
mandate the Municipal Accountant to reconcile  the  RPCPPE  with  accounting
records and investigate discrepancies noted.


      We also reiterate  our  prior  year’s  recommendations  requiring  the
Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory  of  PPE  every  year,
and submit a report on the results thereon.  We further recommend  that  the
AREs of such properties be renewed periodically every three (3) years.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  each  program  and
project  coordinator  to  fast-track  the  implementation  of  programs  and
projects funded by the National Government in accordance with  the  timeline
provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the  Liquidation  Reports
and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal  Engineer
to ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP  in  order
to maximize the  utilization  of  the  fund  and  achieve  desirable  socio-
economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents.

      We also recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  maintain  Subsidiary
Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for  all  disbursements  and
balances of the  fund  and  reconcile  immediately  the  balances  from  the
records of the Budget Officer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit the authority granted  by
the SB and MDRRMF prior to procurement of  vehicle.  Also,  include  in  the
MDRRMFIP all items to be  procured and activities to be undertaken out  of
the Mitigation Fund and Trust Fund and disburse said funds only if found  in
the MDRRMFIP.

We recommend that the Local School Board give equal importance to  all
priority  expenditures,  specifically   on   the   improvement   of   school
facilities, in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget and in  the
utilization of SEF.

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  request  from  the  Provincial
Government of Biliran for a copy of the  required  quarterly  accomplishment
report.     Also, require the concerned official to  prepare  an  integrated
Municipal RYF Plan in accordance with the framework of  the  Provincial  RYF
Plan.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  designated  Solid
Waste Management Officer to follow-up with the NSWMC the immediate  approval
of the municipality’s ten (10) year SWMP.
